reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All 6 gates passed. Gate 1 (Dead Code): Function is actively called from beacon-chain/sync/data_columns_reconstruct.go:71 for P2P reconstruction. Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): Processes DataColumnSidecars from P2P peers (UNTRUSTED), within correct trust boundary. Gate 3 (Code Verification): Code reading is factually correct—lines 79-82 iterate Go map sidecarByIndex without sorting, append to cellsIndices, pass to RecoverCellsAndKZGProofs at line 86. Go spec guarantees randomized map iteration order. Test at fulu__kzg__recover_cells_and_kzg_proofs_test.go:45-57 confirms c-kzg-4844 library expects sorted indices and fails otherwise. Gate 4 (Exploitability): An attacker can send valid DataColumnSidecars via P2P; reconstruction will randomly order indices due to map iteration, causing c-kzg-4844 to fail (test shows nil output for unsorted indices), breaking PeerDAS reconstruction. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e property found in outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W3B4_1771748827.json:149-166, requiring 'is_sorted(cell_indices) AND is_unique(cell_indices)'. Gate 6 (Scope): PeerDAS-specific code in scope. Severity: Original Medium severity is appropriate—Prysm has 31% consensus client share (exceeds 5% threshold for Medium per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json:206), reconstruction failure prevents affected nodes from recovering missing data columns, impacting PeerDAS reliability but not causing consensus splits or slashing.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Recovery cell_indices must be unique and sorted in ascending order.
